behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration proof: this failure appears at the handoff between the PR-changed-file provider and the analyzer input set (e.g., wrong merge of changed paths causes unchanged files to be queued). Integration-brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess is required because a deterministic in-process fake PR diff source and fake analyzer can reproduce the failure; no AI/ML output dependency; no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text only requires 'files changed in the pull request are analyzed' and does not require real GitHub API/network behavior to validate the filtering contract.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the changed-file collector module to the analysis scheduler with an in-memory repo fixture containing changed and unchanged files. Feed a synthetic PR diff list, execute the pipeline stage, and assert analyzer invocations occur only for diff-listed files and never for unchanged paths.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is formatting/grouping logic over finding objects and can fail inside one local function/object boundary without I/O. No proven module-boundary failure is required to catch mis-grouping. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text only requires grouping by severity in output, which can be validated by pure input->output transformation.
behaviors.test_description: Call the comment-render/grouping function with mixed findings and assert output sections are exactly grouped into error/warning/info with correct membership and ordering rules.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration proof: runtime depends on orchestration across file enumeration, rule execution, and result aggregation modules. Integration -> System proof: passing/failing is defined against real wall-clock timeout in GitHub Actions ('within the 10-minute Actions timeout'), which depends on real runner CPU/IO/process behavior and cannot be trusted with in-process mocks alone. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because no AI/ML output quality or non-determinism is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the lint step end-to-end in CI-like execution (same command/path as GitHub Action) against a generated/fixture PR change set of 200 files and assert total elapsed time stays under 10 minutes while still producing findings output.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration proof: resilience requires interaction between per-file execution, failure isolation, and final reporting modules. Integration -> System proof: behavior explicitly says 'linter process crashes', which is subprocess/OS-exit behavior (signal/exit code/pipe failure) that mocks can hide. Integration-brake confirms real subprocess is required; no AI/ML dependency; no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline with the real linter subprocess against a corpus containing one malformed file that triggers a crash and several valid files. Assert the run continues for remaining files, aggregates surviving findings, and posts/finalizes a results payload instead of aborting entirely.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: This is deduplication logic on finding keys (path, line, rule/signature) and can fail within a single local function/object boundary with no I/O. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on output dedup semantics, not on cross-module infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the dedup function with findings containing intentional same-line overlaps from multiple rules and assert only one canonical entry per dedup key is emitted, while distinct findings remain.
